meta.title: A2UI live-agent example — the ladder's last rung: a real LLM emitting A2UI over the wire (prompt → validated stream → rendered surface → human interaction → client message → the agent continues), layered as a deterministic recorded backbone (always-on, gate-covered) + an opt-in dev-only live overlay (server-proxy path + a provisioned client-direct path, both behind the AgentTransport seam), a CONFIG-DRIVEN multi-provider seam (a committed providers.json registry enumerating Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini + models — the SoT for the in-chat provider→model switcher AND the proxy allowlist; Anthropic implemented now, OpenAI/Gemini the immediate next adapter slices) · zero package deps · secret-free deterministic CI · the built/deployed static bundle NEVER bakes a key (the VITE_ inlining footgun) · tools land NESTED under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools (the tools/corpus precedent), standing tests under src/
meta.author: planner (design seat — the live-agent intake, NEXT item 3)
upstream: .claude/docs/specs/a2ui-expert-system.prd.md (PRD-G1 end-to-end generation; PRD-G7 transport; C1 v1.0 conform; C2 zero-dep)
.claude/docs/specs/specs/a2ui-streaming-pipeline.spec.md (v0.2 — SPEC-R2 generation pipeline REALIZED here; R3 stdio/transport; R8 ordering; N1 progressive; N2 zero-dep core)
.claude/docs/specs/llds/a2ui-streaming-pipeline.lld.md (v0.2 — LLD-C2 produce() driver REALIZED here; LLD-C1 codec deferred; the re-point ADR-0067 applied)
.claude/docs/specs/specs/a2ui-expert-harness.spec.md (v0.2 — SPEC-R6 loop CONTRACT this wave realizes PROGRAMMATICALLY; the rubric is authoring-time not runtime)
.claude/docs/specs/llds/a2ui-harness-wiring.lld.md (v0.2.1 §6 — the bounded-loop contract the driver inherits; the named live-agent trigger)
.claude/skills/a2ui-compose/SKILL.md + references/ (the node-idiom grammar the derived system prompt is built from)
packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/renderer/renderer.ts (createRenderer host — ingest/finalize/mount/onClientMessage/dispose — the client surface the demo rides, NOT redesigned)
packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/corpus/retrieve.ts (retrieve(records, query) — few-shot conditioning; pure, over the JUDGED 11-record shard)
packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/corpus/heal.ts + src/renderer/validate.ts (the shared heal + validator the runtime loop uses — no fork)
packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/catalog/default/catalog.json (the SOLE component/prop/function authority the prompt derives from; 19 components)
site/pages/a2ui-stream.ts (the transport/render/round-trip precedent one rung down — createRenderer used exactly as a server transport would; the deterministic-seed + standing-gate pattern)
.claude/docs/adr/0055-a2ui-example-seeds-package-home.md (the seed-shelf home + the examples.test.ts standing gate the backbone rides)
.claude/docs/adr/0062-corpus-packaging-pure-core-subpath-data-home.md (the pure-core / Node-shell / subpath split the tools harness mirrors)
.claude/docs/adr/0067-harness-right-sizing-realized-estate.md (the loop is procedural until THIS wave's programmatic driver; streaming LLD-C2 re-pointed here)
.claude/docs/process.md (rule 1 deterministic→script; generator≠critic — the runtime loop uses gates, the rubric critic stays authoring-time)
meta.seat: DESIGN slices l1-l6 + l16 (SPEC/LLD/ADRs): THIS intake (planner), ratified by the host+Kim on a green gate. BUILD slices: l7 (the AgentTransport seam) + l8/l11 (recorded backbone + session reducer, PURE) land under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent with their STANDING TESTS under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent (vitest+tsc); l13/l15/l19 (live-overlay transports + the site page + the in-chat switcher): site seat under site/ (check:site typecheck; no vitest; MANUAL interaction/live acceptance); l9-l10/l17/l18 (the Node loop driver + catalog-derived prompt + the AgentProvider adapters + providers.json) + l12 (the dev-proxy plugin): a tools/harness seat (a2ui-builder-adjacent, dev-scoped, zero package deps, type-stripped execution; pure parts typechecked+tested transitively via the src tests that import them); l14 (the secret-free standing gates incl. the build-key-safety source-grep): the same test-owning seat under src/, gate-covered by npm test + a manual build-grep. Live-overlay slices (l12/l13/l17-live-HTTP) carry MANUAL run-time acceptance (a real key from .env), NEVER a CI gate. Gates per slice named in each accept.
meta.deferred: the formal JSONL codec (streaming LLD-C1 — the demo does line-framing in the transport; the codec lands with the streaming producer wave unless the reviewer pulls it forward) · AG-UI / A2A / MCP transport adapters (streaming LLD-C3..C6 — the demo's stdio-equivalent is the browser↔proxy text stream; other transports stay deferred to the streaming producer wave) · the client-direct browser transport (BrowserDirectTransport) is DESIGNED behind the SAME AgentTransport seam and PROVISIONED (VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY exists in the gitignored .env), CORS now HOST-VERIFIED viable-but-officially-dangerous — so it stays the deferred/dev-only arm gated on the dev-only-tree-shake guarantee; the proxy overlay is the primary live path this wave · the OpenAI + Gemini provider ADAPTERS are the IMMEDIATE NEXT SLICES (Kim-directed): their config entries land NOW in providers.json (l18), their adapter modules (openai.ts/gemini.ts under the same AgentProvider seam, l17) come next — host-verify their streaming contracts BEFORE those slices (Anthropic-first means they are non-blocking now, NOT indefinite YAGNI) · promoting the session reducer to a package ./agent subpath (only if the reviewer finds it a genuine reusable runtime primitive — a follow-up ADR against 0069, not required to prove the wave) · corpus-lift measurement / an eval facet (corpus LLD-C8/C12 keep their triggers; this demo SHOWS retrieval conditioning, it does not MEASURE lift)
meta.env: A gitignored repo-root .env (verified untracked, .gitignore:27) provisions SIX keys — server-side + browser-exposed (VITE_) variants for THREE providers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY · OPENAI_API_KEY/VITE_OPENAI_API_KEY · GEMINI_API_KEY/VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY. So BOTH live paths are provisioned for LOCAL dev, not hypothetical: the proxy reads the non-prefixed process.env[<envKey>] (envKey per provider from providers.json); a client-direct path would read import.meta.env.VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Kim provisioning BOTH signals the layered combo AND the multi-provider intent. CRITICAL FOOTGUN (a hard design invariant, NOT a host-verify): Vite INLINES every VITE_* var into the client bundle at BUILD time (npm run build), so a VITE_ key referenced by built code is BAKED into any deployed static artifact = a real key exposure. The design GUARANTEES the deterministic backbone is what npm run build ships + what the standing gate covers, and every live overlay is dev-only-guarded (import.meta.env.DEV + dynamic import) so vite build tree-shakes it out — no VITE_ key reference survives into dist/. The committed providers.json holds env-var NAMES + public endpoints/model-ids ONLY — never a secret. This tightens Fork F and is an explicit AC (l14) + ADR-0069 consequence; the mechanism generalizes to N providers UNCHANGED.
meta.hostVerify: ALL THREE original flags DISCHARGED by the host 2026-07-04 (scratchpad/live-agent-host-verified-facts.md) — folded in as RESOLVED: (1) Anthropic Messages streaming HOST-VERIFIED — POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages, headers x-api-key + anthropic-version:2023-06-01 + content-type, body stream:true; SSE message_start→(content_block_start→content_block_delta*→content_block_stop)+→message_delta→message_stop; text at delta.text where delta.type==='text_delta'; mid-stream errors = event:error (surface, don't drop); (2) client-direct CORS HOST-VERIFIED — supported but Anthropic officially DANGEROUS (dangerouslyAllowBrowser / anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access; 'exposes your secret API credentials in client-side code') → CONFIRMS proxy-default + BrowserDirect stays deferred/dev-only-guarded; (3) A2A continuity HOST-VERIFIED — Message carries messageId/contextId/taskId, TASK_STATE_INPUT_REQUIRED is the non-terminal interrupt, resume via a normal SendMessage echoing taskId+contextId (NO bespoke envelope) → ADR-0072 CITES this as the conformance target (design already compatible). NEW per-slice flags (non-blocking now, Anthropic-first): OpenAI + Gemini streaming contracts are host-verify-BEFORE-those-adapter-slices. The upstream-SSE parsing stays isolated PER-PROVIDER module (SPEC-N5), so each provider's contract touches one file.
meta.notes: This is the FIRST programmatic realization of the SPEC-R6 compose→validate loop (harness LLD §6 says so by name) and the concrete realization of streaming LLD-C2's produce() driver. The design's spine is the AgentTransport isolation seam: the page/client is transport-SHAPE-INDEPENDENT (l7), so the recorded backbone (l8), the live proxy client (l13), and the provisioned client-direct transport all satisfy one interface — if Kim picks a different live mechanism ONLY that transport (+ the proxy) churn, not the SPEC or the page. The AgentProvider seam (l17) is a SECOND seam under that: stream({model,system,messages,signal})→AsyncIterable<string> the driver injects, ONE isolated module per provider, config-driven (l18 providers.json = the SoT for the l19 in-chat switcher AND the l12 proxy allowlist); Anthropic implemented now, OpenAI/Gemini the immediate next adapter slices (Kim-directed — config entries present now, NOT indefinite YAGNI). PLACEMENT (B2): tools land NESTED under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent (the tools/corpus precedent) — NOT the repo root; those modules are type-stripped-execution + typechecked TRANSITIVELY only when imported by an included src file; the STANDING TESTS live under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent/ (the vitest+tsc include) and reach out into ../../tools/agent. Every ADR here is status:proposed; the host ratifies with Kim on a green gate. ADR numbers = 0069-0073.

nodes.label: Live-agent example wave (the A2UI ladder's last rung — NEXT item 3)
SPEC .claude/docs/specs/specs/a2ui-live-agent.spec.md (this intake)
nodes.accept: the file exists with Purpose · Definitions · Requirements (R#, each with PRD/streaming/harness trace + acceptance criteria) · Non-functionals (N#) · Typed contracts (the AgentTransport + Turn/Session shapes) · Traceability; every acceptance criterion is a CHECKABLE predicate (a command, a standing test name, a grep, or a named manual-run) never 'done when good'; it REFINES the PRD (PRD-G1 end-to-end generation, PRD-G7 transport) and REALIZES streaming SPEC-R2 + harness SPEC-R6 by ID (cross-referenced, not duplicated — the loop CONTRACT stays harness §6, the producer behavior stays streaming R2; this SPEC owns integration + demo-shape + backbone + security posture); harness_checks.py spec exits 0 on the file; all cross-references resolve

nodes.label: LLD skeleton .claude/docs/specs/llds/a2ui-live-agent.lld.md (this intake)
nodes.accept: the file exists with a §1 component map (LLD-C#, each with Implements SPEC-R# + File + Scope[site|tools|package] + State) · a §2 file/module map partitioning every artifact into site/ (page+UI), tools/ (dev proxy + Node driver + prompt derivation + recorded transcript) and package (NONE this wave — the surface stays exactly .`/`./examples`/`./corpus`) · a §3 deterministic-backbone contract (the recorded-transcript shape + the standing-gate wiring) · a §4 round-trip state machine (idle→generating→streaming→awaiting-interaction→next-turn) · a §5 error/edge table; every C# cites a SPEC-R#; the streaming LLD-C2 produce() driver is realized concretely here; harness_checks.py lld exits 0; cross-references resolve

nodes.label: ADR-0069 — Fork A (headline): the live-demo shape + security posture — layered deterministic recorded backbone (always-on, gate-covered, what build ships) + an OPT-IN, DEV-ONLY live overlay (server-proxy primary + a provisioned client-direct arm), both behind the AgentTransport isolation seam (absorbs Fork C retrieval-placement + Fork F backbone-mechanism + the VITE_ build-key-safety invariant)
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0; Status: proposed (never self-ratified); indexed in adr/README.md; Decision states: (a) the AgentTransport interface is the isolation seam — the page consumes ONLY it, so backbone↔proxy↔client-direct swap with no page change; (b) the deterministic recorded transcript is the DEFAULT + the ONLY thing CI exercises + what npm run build ships; (c) the primary live overlay is a dev-server Vite middleware PROXY holding the key from process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, calling the Anthropic REST API with PLAIN FETCH (no LLM SDK, no package dep), streaming validated A2UI JSONL back; (d) retrieval + the loop live proxy-side (Node — fs-store loads the shard) [Fork C]; (e) the backbone mechanism is a RECORDED TRANSCRIPT not a hand-mocked agent, gate-covered by a packages-tree test [Fork F]; (f) a client-direct BrowserDirectTransport (reads VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, runs the pure loop client-side) is PROVISIONED behind the same seam; CORS is HOST-VERIFIED viable-but-officially-dangerous, so it stays the deferred/dev-only arm; (g) THE VITE_ INLINING FOOTGUN is an explicit consequence + AC: Vite bakes VITE_* into the build bundle, so EVERY live overlay is dev-only-guarded (import.meta.env.DEV + dynamic import) and vite build tree-shakes it out — the built/deployed static bundle contains NO key and NO VITE_ key reference (proven by l14's build-key-safety source-grep gate); Alternatives weighs (a) proxy vs (b) client-direct vs (c) recorded-only vs (d) layered and picks layered (the .env provisioning of BOTH server + VITE variants signals the layered combo) with the proxy as the safe default; Repairs names the streaming LLD-C2 concrete realization + this decomp

nodes.label: ADR-0070 — Fork B: the runtime loop scope — generate → heal+validate → self-correct (maxRounds 3) → VALIDATE-THEN-STREAM → render, with NO runtime rubric-critic round (the a2ui-payload rubric + a2ui-reviewer stay authoring/eval-time)
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0; Status: proposed; indexed; Decision: the runtime loop is the harness SPEC-R6 contract MINUS the critic round — the deterministic gate (shared heal+validate) is the whole runtime verifier; the rubric applies at authoring time (the recorded transcript's payloads were graded ≥4 before commit; the retrieved corpus is already tier-2 judged); and the loop produces a FULLY-VALIDATED payload per turn BEFORE streaming its lines to the browser (provable validity, PRD-G4, precedes any paint — the alternative 'stream-raw-then-validate-at-finalize' is named + rejected as default for risking an invalid partial surface, offered as a variant); Consequences names the first-paint latency trade the validate-then-stream choice buys; Alternatives weighs runtime-critic (rejected: no Task tool / no seat-dispatch from a web demo, and generator≠critic is an authoring discipline) vs stream-live

nodes.label: ADR-0071 — Fork D: the machine system prompt is DERIVED from catalog.json + the a2ui-compose grammar, drift-gated — never hand-maintained
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0; Status: proposed; indexed; Decision: buildSystemPrompt(catalog, exemplars) composes THREE parts — (1) the stable node-idiom grammar + the three message kinds + the loop, sourced from the a2ui-compose references (cross-referenced, kept DRY), (2) the catalog INVENTORY derived at run time from catalog.json (the sole authority — never a hand-listed component set), (3) the few-shot block from retrieve() over the judged shard; the DRIFT GATE is a standing test asserting the derived prompt's component/prop inventory EQUALS catalog.json's keys (adding a catalog row without regenerating fails) — the strongest anti-drift form (derive-don't-duplicate + a coverage assertion, the corpus-data/examples-gate precedent); Consequences: the prompt can never name a component the catalog lacks by construction; Alternatives weighs a hand-maintained prompt (rejected: drifts silently as the catalog grows — PRD-G6) vs deriving

nodes.label: ADR-0072 — Fork E: the multi-turn client-round-trip session model — a client message (action | functionResponse | error) becomes the next turn's user input; a stateless proxy; surface continuity via the existing host
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0; Status: proposed; indexed; Decision: a Session is an ordered turn list (user=intent or framed-client-message, assistant=emitted A2UI stream) — the standard Messages-API array; a client message from onClientMessage is FRAMED as a structured user turn (action name + context + dataModel when sendDataModel/wantResponse; a functionResponse feeds the awaited value; an error feeds the validation failure so the agent self-corrects on the NEXT turn); turn N+1's payload targets the SAME surfaceId (updateComponents/updateDataModel patch — 'the agent continues in place') OR a new surface, both already handled by the host; the PROXY IS STATELESS per request — the browser holds the turn history and sends it each turn (no server session store); a demo-level max-turns cap guards a runaway agent; Alternatives weighs a stateful proxy session-store (rejected: needless server state for a static-site demo) and a bespoke on-wire session envelope (deferred to the host-verify A2A-continuity flag — kept in the producer layer so conformance never touches the renderer)

nodes.label: the AgentTransport interface — the transport-shape isolation seam the page consumes (site/lib or tools/agent, pure zero-dep TS)
nodes.accept: an AgentTransport interface exists (turn(input) → AsyncIterable<string> of A2UI JSONL lines, plus the Turn/Session/{provider,model} input types) with zero runtime deps, at packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/agent-transport.ts (re-exported for the site via a site/lib shim if the site tsconfig needs it in-tree); the page module imports ONLY this interface (a grep proves the page has no direct fetch/proxy/RecordedTransport-internal import); a type-only contract test under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent typechecks under npm run check; the interface is documented as the single swap point (backbone ↔ proxy ↔ client-direct)

nodes.label: RecordedTransport + the committed recorded transcript (the deterministic backbone: replay a real captured turn sequence; reuse a shelf seed as the turn-1 payload so payload validity is already gate-covered)
nodes.accept: packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/{recorded-transport.ts,transcript.ts}: a RecordedTransport implements AgentTransport by replaying a committed transcript (turns: prompt → turn-1 A2UI stream [reusing an existing shelf seed, e.g. generativeFormSeed, so examples.test.ts already proves its validity] → the expected client message → turn-2 A2UI stream); the transcript is DATA (no live logic); the l14 round-trip gate under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent imports + drives it through a real createRenderer() (which typechecks+runs it — the transitive-coverage path); npm test green; no network, no key, fully deterministic

nodes.label: the Node loop driver produce() — streaming LLD-C2 realized: retrieve → generate → heal+validate → self-correct (maxRounds 3) → emit a VALIDATED stream (dev/CI-scoped tools, zero package deps)
nodes.accept: packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/produce.ts realizes the harness SPEC-R6 loop programmatically over the SHARED heal + validateA2ui + retrieve surfaces (no fork, N3 parity); the STANDING unit test lives under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent/ (vitest+tsc include) and drives produce() with a STUB AgentProvider (yields first-invalid-then-valid fragments, no live model) proving: only a validated stream is emitted, self-correction feeds the validator's failures back, and exhaustion halts-and-reports at maxRounds=3 (deterministic, secret-free); npm run check + npm test green (the pure driver is typechecked+run via the src test that imports it; the module itself also runs standalone via node --experimental-strip-types); the real model call is INJECTED as deps.provider (an l17 AgentProvider) so the driver is provider-agnostic + testable; the same driver is reusable by the proxy (server-side) AND a client-direct overlay (browser-side, pure surfaces)

nodes.label: buildSystemPrompt — the catalog-derived, few-shot-conditioned machine prompt (grammar from a2ui-compose + inventory from catalog.json + exemplars from retrieve())
nodes.accept: packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/system-prompt.ts composes buildSystemPrompt(catalog, exemplars): the catalog-inventory section is DERIVED from catalog.json at run time (never hand-listed); it accepts the retrieve() top-k exemplars as the few-shot block; pure, zero-dep, Node-type-stripped; consumed by l9 (the driver); its drift gate (l14b) lives under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent and imports buildSystemPrompt (typecheck+run via that src test)

nodes.label: the session/round-trip reducer — client message → next-turn user input; the turn-history model (pure zero-dep)
nodes.accept: packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/session.ts: a pure reducer maps (session, clientMessage) → the next Turn input (framing action/functionResponse/error per ADR-0072) and appends turns; zero deps; a STANDING unit test under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent proves each client-message arm frames correctly and that a functionResponse/error/action each produce a distinct next-turn user content (npm test green); consumed by the page (l15) and asserted by the backbone gate (l14a)

nodes.label: the dev-server Vite middleware proxy plugin — the key-holder (process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), hosts the loop driver + retrieval + the l17 model-client, streams validated A2UI JSONL back (tools, dev-only, zero package deps)
nodes.accept: a Vite plugin (configureServer middleware, added to the site/dev config WITHOUT touching the zero-dep package, and dev-only so vite build output is untouched) at packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/dev-proxy-plugin.ts exposes a dev-only endpoint that: reads the requested {provider,model} from the request, VALIDATES the PAIR against the l18 providers.json ALLOWLIST (the model must be in that provider's models; an unknown pair is REJECTED — never trusts an arbitrary client value) and DEGRADES an allowlisted-but-implemented:false provider to backbone-only (never an unhandled crash — defensive dispatch), reads the matched provider's key from process.env[<config.envKey>] (never a literal — a grep proves no committed key), loads the shard via fs-store + retrieve(), runs the l9 driver with deps.provider = the matched l17 adapter (plain fetch — no LLM SDK anywhere, a grep proves no @anthropic-ai/sdk import), and streams the VALIDATED payload's JSONL lines back; with no key set the endpoint returns a graceful 'no key — backbone only' signal; MANUAL acceptance: a real dev run with the provider key set (from the .env) streams a real validated payload that renders (NOT a CI gate — the live call never gates); the process.env key is server-side so it is NEVER inlined into any client bundle; the allowlist-validation logic (config→env-key routing, reject-unknown) is pure and typechecked+tested transitively via a src test

nodes.label: the live-overlay transport(s) implementing AgentTransport — LiveProxyTransport (browser→proxy, primary) + the DESIGNED, provisioned client-direct BrowserDirectTransport (build gated on CORS-verify); both dev-only-guarded so build tree-shakes them
nodes.accept: a LiveProxyTransport implements AgentTransport by POSTing a turn (with the client-held turn history) to the l12 proxy endpoint and yielding the A2UI JSONL lines it streams back (the browser sees only clean A2UI — the upstream SSE parsing is the l17 model-client's, isolated in ONE place); the page swaps RecordedTransport→LiveProxyTransport with no other change (proves l7's seam); the overlay transport module is reached ONLY via a dev-only dynamic import guarded by import.meta.env.DEV so vite build tree-shakes it (no live transport, and if browser-direct, no VITE_ key reference, survives into dist/ — the l14 build-key-safety gate proves it); MANUAL acceptance rides l12's live run; type-checks under npm run check. The client-direct BrowserDirectTransport (reads import.meta.env.VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, runs the pure produce() loop + l17 model-client entirely in the browser) is DESIGNED behind the same seam but its build is deferred pending the host-verify CORS flag

nodes.label: the secret-free standing gates — the deterministic-backbone round-trip test + the prompt-drift test + the BUILD-KEY-SAFETY gate (the built bundle bakes no key)
nodes.accept: the standing gates live under packages/agent-ui/a2ui/src/live-agent/ (the vitest+tsc include) and reach out into ../../tools/agent (the site-canon reach-out precedent — a src test importing a tools module, which also transitively typechecks that module); npm test green with (a) a round-trip test driving the committed transcript through a real createRenderer() host + the session reducer, asserting turn-1 renders, a simulated interaction emits the expected client message, and turn-2 ingests + updates the surface — deterministic, no network/key; (b) a prompt-drift test asserting buildSystemPrompt's derived component/prop inventory EQUALS Object.keys(catalog.components)/each row's props (a planted catalog row absent from the prompt fails) — the l5/ADR-0071 gate; (c) the BUILD-KEY-SAFETY source-grep test asserting every import.meta.env.VITE_* reference lives ONLY inside a live-overlay module reached via a dev-only (import.meta.env.DEV) dynamic import — never in the page's static import graph — PLUS a MANUAL 'vite build then grep dist/ for the key patterns → zero hits' acceptance (the npm-run-size manual-gate precedent, ADR-0040 §3); and (d) a providers.json shape test (l18); all deterministic tests run under npm run check + npm test with zero secret; the import-layering tripwire must permit a src TEST → tools import (tests already import ../examples; the tripwire targets src/** NON-test imports) — VERIFY at build, and if it objects, fall back to committing the transcript/config as src fixtures (named in the LLD)

nodes.label: the site page a2ui-live.ts + a2ui-live.html + the dual-TOC wiring (the visible proof, riding the l14 gates the way a2ui-stream rides examples.test.ts)
nodes.accept: site/a2ui-live.html + site/pages/a2ui-live.ts render a live-agent demo: a prompt input, the l19 provider→model switcher, the streamed surface (createRenderer + mount), the client→server log, and a turn history; it defaults to the RecordedTransport backbone (deterministic, works on the built static site) and offers a live overlay ONLY under import.meta.env.DEV via a dynamic import of the l13 transport (so vite build tree-shakes the overlay + any VITE_ reference out); every displayed fact derives from the transport output (shown ≡ produced — the a2ui-stream discipline); it is wired into the dual TOC (site/main.ts); typechecked by check:site (site/** include; NOT vitest — site tests don't run, so its proof rides the l14 src gates); npm run check + check:site green; the page rides the l14 standing gates (it invents no parallel check); the built output (dist/) contains NO key and NO hardcoded live endpoint and NO VITE_ key reference (N2, proven by l14c)

nodes.label: ADR-0073 — Fork G: the CONFIG-DRIVEN multi-provider seam — an AgentProvider stream() seam + a committed providers.json registry (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini + models) as the SoT for the in-chat switcher AND the proxy allowlist; Anthropic implemented NOW, OpenAI/Gemini the immediate NEXT adapter slices (Kim-ratified — REVERSES the prior YAGNI stance)
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0; Status: proposed; indexed; RETITLED (no 'Claude-only first cut / YAGNI'); Decision: the driver injects a provider-neutral AgentProvider.stream({model,system,messages,signal}) → AsyncIterable<string> text-fragment seam (the l9 injection point NAMED); a committed providers.json (l18) enumerates ALL THREE providers + their models + an implemented:boolean flag NOW (the menu AND the allowlist; implemented reconciles 'lists all three' with 'Anthropic-first' — the switcher disables + the proxy degrades unimplemented); ONE isolated module PER PROVIDER (l17) owns its endpoint+auth+stream framing — Anthropic implemented NOW, OpenAI/Gemini config-present-now / adapter-next-slice (NOT indefinite deferral); an in-chat provider→model switcher (l19) renders from the config + persists to localStorage; the proxy (l12) VALIDATES the requested {provider,model} against the config allowlist and routes to process.env[envKey]; Consequences: the driver stays provider-agnostic + stub-testable, a new provider is a new module + a config row; Alternatives — 'build a provider abstraction now (config + all three enumerated)' is CHOSEN (forcing context = Kim's /intent-extract directive + the in-chat switcher CONSUMER), and 'Claude-only YAGNI, no config, no 2nd provider' is now the REJECTED alternative (a real consumer + directive exist — the YAGNI premise no longer holds); Repairs names the SPEC-R11/R12 rework + LLD-C10 per-provider + this decomp

nodes.label: the AgentProvider adapters — a provider-neutral stream() seam + one isolated module PER PROVIDER (Anthropic NOW; openai.ts/gemini.ts the immediate next slices), each owning its endpoint+auth+SSE framing (SPEC-N5 isolation per-module)
nodes.accept: packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/providers/anthropic.ts implements AgentProvider.stream({model,system,messages,signal}) → AsyncIterable<string> over PLAIN fetch to POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages (headers x-api-key + anthropic-version:2023-06-01 + content-type; body stream:true; yields delta.text where delta.type==='text_delta'; completes on message_stop; surfaces event:error mid-stream — the HOST-VERIFIED 2026-07-04 contract) — no LLM SDK (a grep proves no @anthropic-ai/sdk import); the SSE-chunk→text-fragment parse is SPLIT OUT as a PURE function fixture-tested deterministically (a captured SSE response → expected fragments, no network) — the fetch arm is MANUAL live acceptance; it is the SINGLE place Anthropic's SSE is parsed into text (SPEC-N5 — one file per provider); the key is passed IN (process.env[envKey] server-side / import.meta.env.VITE_ client-side) never read at module scope; a stub AgentProvider drives l9's loop test with no live model; openai.ts + gemini.ts are the IMMEDIATE NEXT SLICES (their config rows land now in l18; host-verify their streaming contracts BEFORE those slices); the pure adapter-dispatch (provider id → module) is typechecked+tested transitively via the src loop test, the live HTTP arm is type-stripped-execution + MANUAL acceptance

nodes.label: the provider config registry — a committed providers.json (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini + models, NO secrets) + a typed loader: the SINGLE source of truth for BOTH the in-chat switcher menu AND the proxy allowlist
nodes.accept: packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/agent/providers.json is committed with shape { defaultProvider, providers: { <id>: { label, envKey, endpoint, defaultModel, models:[{id,label}], implemented } } }: Anthropic seeded with claude-opus-4-8/claude-sonnet-5/claude-fable-5 (defaultModel claude-sonnet-5, defaultProvider anthropic, implemented:true); OpenAI + Gemini entries STUBBED with their public model-ids + endpoints + envKey NAMES (OPENAI_API_KEY/GEMINI_API_KEY) and implemented:false; it holds env-var NAMES + public endpoints/model-ids ONLY — a grep proves NO secret/key VALUE in the file; providers-config.ts exposes PURE validation/lookup/degrade helpers over an already-parsed config (the Node reader does readFileSync+JSON.parse like catalog.json; the browser switcher reads it via a Vite JSON import); a standing src test asserts the file parses, every provider carries envKey/endpoint/defaultModel/implemented, defaultModel ∈ models, defaultProvider ∈ providers AND is implemented; consumed by l19 (menu) and l12 (allowlist) — one SoT, no second list

nodes.label: the in-chat provider→model switcher — dropdowns rendered from providers.json, default from defaultProvider/defaultModel, live-overridable, persisted to localStorage, sends {provider,model} per request (site/)
nodes.accept: a site/ switcher UI (dogfooded ui-* controls, DEV-ONLY — part of the live-overlay UI, so the static backbone-only build has no switcher; reached via the same import.meta.env.DEV guard as the transports) renders provider→model dropdowns FROM the l18 config via a Vite JSON import (never a hand-listed menu — a config row added surfaces automatically), DISABLES implemented:false providers (a visible 'coming soon' roadmap, NOT selectable, so it never offers what the proxy can't serve), defaults from defaultProvider/defaultModel, is live-overridable, persists the selection to localStorage (restored on reload), and passes {provider,model} into every transport turn; typechecked by check:site; MANUAL interaction acceptance (dropdown → next live turn uses the new model); no key touched (the switcher names providers/models, never keys)

actions.label: state why/what/acceptance for the live-agent example as an execution contract (integration + demo-shape + backbone + security), refining the PRD and realizing the streaming/harness contracts by ID
add the how: the module map (site/tools/package), the deterministic-backbone contract, and the round-trip state machine
decide + record the live-demo shape and light-security posture — isolate the transport, layer backbone + overlay, place retrieval + the loop with the key-holder, pick the backbone mechanism
decide + record the runtime loop scope — deterministic gate only at runtime, validate-then-stream, the rubric stays authoring-time
decide + record deriving the machine prompt from the catalog + grammar and drift-gating it
decide + record how a human interaction becomes the agent's next turn and how the session evolves the surface
give the page ONE transport-shape-independent seam so backbone / proxy / client-direct are interchangeable
prove the whole wiring (render + round-trip) WITHOUT a live call — a deterministic recorded backbone
let a real (or stubbed) generator produce ONLY a validated stream via the bounded loop, programmatically
condition generation on the catalog + the judged corpus without the prompt drifting from the catalog
turn a rendered surface's interaction into the agent's next input (the agent continues)
hold the key the browser cannot, call the model, and stream validated A2UI back — dev-only, SDK-free
let the browser consume the live stream through the same seam the backbone uses
keep CI deterministic + secret-free while still gate-covering the backbone round-trip and the prompt-catalog coverage
make the whole loop visible + interactive on the site, riding the standing gates (no parallel check)
decide + record the config-driven multi-provider seam — a registry of all three, Anthropic now, OpenAI/Gemini next slices, an in-chat switcher + a proxy allowlist
call a real model behind a provider-neutral seam, one isolated module per provider, upstream-format parsing per-provider
hold ONE committed source of truth (no secrets) for the provider/model menu AND the proxy allowlist
let a human pick provider+model in-chat, persisted, and carry it into each turn

edges.why: the AgentTransport isolation seam IS ADR-0069's core decision — its shape/existence encodes the fork (authoring the seam before ratification pre-empts the host, the corpus/harness precedent)
the recorded-transcript backbone mechanism (over a hand-mocked agent) is ADR-0069's Fork-F consequence
the dev-proxy-as-key-holder overlay shape (plain fetch, retrieval + loop proxy-side) is ADR-0069's decision (incl. Fork C placement)
the driver realizes the runtime loop scope ADR-0070 pins (deterministic gate only, validate-then-stream, maxRounds 3)
buildSystemPrompt's derive-from-catalog + few-shot shape is ADR-0071's decision
the session reducer's client-message-to-next-turn framing is ADR-0072's decision
RecordedTransport implements the AgentTransport interface — the type must exist to implement
LiveProxyTransport implements the AgentTransport interface — the type must exist to implement
the page consumes AgentTransport (and only it) — the seam must exist before the page is written to it
the driver imports buildSystemPrompt to condition each generation — a direct build dependency
the dev proxy hosts + runs the loop driver (with a plain-fetch generate()) server-side
the AgentProvider.stream() seam + the per-provider-module shape + Anthropic-now/OpenAI-Gemini-next scope is ADR-0073's decision
the config-registry-as-SoT (menu + allowlist) enumerating all three providers is ADR-0073's decision (reverses the prior YAGNI)
the in-chat provider→model switcher is ADR-0073's ratified consumer — the reason the config exists now
the proxy dispatches to the matched l17 adapter as the driver's provider dep — the adapter must exist to route to
the proxy validates {provider,model} against the l18 registry allowlist and reads process.env[envKey] from it — the config must exist to validate against
the switcher renders its provider/model menu FROM the l18 config (one SoT, no hand-listed second menu)
the page hosts the in-chat switcher and threads its {provider,model} selection into each transport turn
the browser transport targets the proxy's endpoint + streamed-response contract — it needs the proxy's request/response shape defined
the deterministic-backbone gate drives the committed recorded transcript through the real host
the backbone round-trip gate asserts the session reducer's framing of the transcript's client message
the prompt-drift gate asserts over buildSystemPrompt's derived inventory vs catalog.json
the page's DEFAULT transport is the recorded backbone (works on the built static site, no proxy)
the page uses the session reducer to feed interactions back through the transport
the page's live overlay is the LiveProxyTransport, swapped in behind the seam
the page rides the standing gates as its visible proof (a2ui-stream↔examples.test.ts precedent) — it invents no parallel check, so the gates precede it
